title: ISic001578. I.Sicily inscription 001578
body:
Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/F66S4X3M
1[---]ΜΑΛ[---]ΔΡΥΜΑ

Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/F66S4X3M
1[---]ΜΑΛ[---]ΔΡΥΜΑ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

Text of IGPalermo

translation

commentary

The text is very difficult to read and the interpretation much disputed, although Garbini was confident that it is not Punic. Guarducci and Garbini debated the presence of various letter forms, whether Megaran or from Selinunte, and Guarducci speculated that it was a non-Greek inscription in Greek letters. It seems most likely to be a damaged and difficult to read funerary inscription.

bibliography

  • Gabrici NSA 1917 344 no.4 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001392
  • Manni Piraino 1963 152 n.33 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001781
  • Garbini 1964-1965 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001968
  • Guarducci 1964-1965 487 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001927
  • Manni Piraino 1973 104 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000531

There is some debate over the letter forms, due to the poor state of the stone, and whether various epichoric letters are present.

Line 1 30-45 Interlineation line 1 to 2
